Subject: Commissions
Full Comments: 02/22/2006 Gary Curti: Here is Bob''s letter to John Phelps, to Shirley Carethers, to Steve Blum, to me, and to Jennifer Hockey: Dear John, I have a very big concern and I hope you can help me. Several months ago I sent you an e-mail about it. I was appalled that the commission department had sent out an $18,000 annuity commission to someone in my downline for a $200,000 annuity that had been written on a family member before the free look period was even up. The agent, a 21 year old, had written it on his grandfather who, as soon as the commission was paid, had cancelled the contract and gotten his money back. The agent had called the commission department and was told not to send the commission back because the chargeback would be taken from future commissions. I was frantic and notified collections to please demand the money immediately, which evidently they did by letter, but to no avail. John, I have done all I know to do. I have written the agent. I called and spoke with him twice, both times being assured that he would return the money. I have e-mailed him. Now, he won''t even answer my calls. I don''t know what to do, but I know that an $18,000 chargeback to me would be devastating. I, of course, paid back the commission I received. And I did have the recruiting agent send back his commission the first week as well. Once again let me stress that the business was family business, and the commission was sent out before the free look was up, which I never, never would have authorized. And then the agent was initially told not to worry about sending it back because future commissions would handle it, which was ludicrous. The agent, by the way, has since then been terminated. The agent, by the way, is Lorenza Smith, agent # 000206241. In my opinion he should have been arrested for fraud, but, of course, I have no authority to do anything. John, I am at my wits end worrying about this situation. I am helpless. Can you help me. In Christ, Bob I called him to let him know that OMFN has sent letters from legal and referred it out to a collections agency, and will eventually recoup the cost from the AGA or MGA plus 25%. Any moneys recovered are put off against whomever is charged back for commissions paid to Lorenza Smith.

Subject: PHS
Full Comments: Policy Number: L9051843 11/08/2006 Jaime Prillaman: We requested a withdrawal for one of our clients (Carol Hirsch) from her F&G NQ account (account L9051849 confirmed the request was received on 10/12). On 10/27 I called to check the status of the withdrawal and it was expedited as it was the 10th business days from the date of receipt of the request (7 ? 10 business days to process w/d requests). I called F&G today (11/8) to confirm check had been sent via overnight as had been requested and found that the check had been cut last night and should be sent today via overnight. I then found that the check had been voided and re-issued as they?d sent the check to the wrong customer. (The original check had mailed 10/30 so they had expedited the check as I?d been told.) We have another request for withdrawal for the same client from her F&G IRA account (L9051843 confirmed the request was received on 11/3). I asked if there was any way that an expedite could be put on this check due to their error regarding the other. I was told that they could not as it?s not passed their deadline for processing (regardless of what happened with the other withdrawal as it was for another account so doesn?t apply to this IRA account). Call me crazy, but wouldn?t one think a company would be interested in doing something to re-instill client confidence in their accounts? Sent request to Project Resolution and Elaine.
